data.title: Buljol
data.cook_source:
---
title: Buljol
servings: 4
tags:
  - Caribbean
  - Fish
  - Bread
---

= Buljol =

Place @salt cod{1%lb} in cold water and shred into thin strands. Squeeze to remove salt, place in fresh water. Repeat 5–7 times; remove as much water as possible on last rinse.

Dice @onion{1}(medium), finely mince @garlic{4%cloves}, dice @habanero pepper{1}(seeds removed). Mix into the cod with @lemon juice{3%tbsp} (or lime/vinegar) and @olive oil{4%tbsp}. Season with @salt{} and @black pepper{}. Let sit at least ~{3%hours} or ideally overnight.

Slice tomatoes, eggs, avocado or bell pepper to garnish. Serve with crusty bread or boiled potatoes.

= Bread =

Combine @active dry yeast{2 1/4%tsp}, @sugar{1%tsp}, and @warm water{1 1/4%cups} in a large bowl. Add @all-purpose flour{2 1/2%cups} to @3 1/2%cups and @kosher salt{1 1/4%tsp}. Mix until sticky dough forms. Cover and let rise ~{1%hour} until doubled.

Tip onto floured surface, fold edges until tight. Shape into a round. Rest in floured bowl ~{30%minutes} while oven preheats.

Preheat #Dutch oven{} or #cast iron{} to 450°F. Bake dough seam side up ~{30%minutes}, remove lid, bake ~{10%minutes} more until golden.

data.title: Chocolate Chip Pecan Cookies
data.cook_source:
---
title: Chocolate Chip Pecan Cookies
servings: 12
tags:
  - Cookies
  - Dessert
  - Baking
---

Melt @unsalted butter{1.25%cups} in a medium #saucepan{}, then set aside to cool.

In a medium bowl, whisk together @cake flour{1.5%cups}, @all-purpose flour{2%cups}, @kosher salt{1.5%tsp}, @cornstarch{2%tsp}, and @baking soda{1/2%tsp}. Set aside.

In another medium bowl whisk @light brown sugar{1.25%cups} and @granulated sugar{1/2%cup} with the melted butter until creamy. Add @eggs{2} one at a time, then @egg yolks{3}, mixing fully after each. Whisk in @vanilla extract{2%tsp}.

Mix in the flour mixture until fully incorporated. Fold in @pecans{2%cups}(roughly chopped) and @dark chocolate{1%lb}(roughly chopped). Cover and refrigerate for at least ~{1%hour}.

Preheat the #oven{} to 425°F (220°C). Divide dough into roughly 6 ounce pieces, roll into tight balls. Place 4 cookies on a parchment-lined #baking sheet{}. Bake for ~{10%minutes} or up to ~{13%minutes}, until lightly browned outside and slightly underdone inside. Cool on a wire rack.

data.title: Chocolate Mousse
data.cook_source:
---
title: Chocolate Mousse
servings: 8
tags:
  - Dessert
  - Chocolate
  - Cake
---

= Chocolate Cake Layer =

Sift @flour{1%cup}, @baking powder{1/2%tsp}, @baking soda{1/2%tsp}, and @unsweetened cocoa powder{2%tbsp} into a bowl. Add @sugar{3/4%cup} and @salt{1%pinch}.

In another bowl whisk @egg{1}, @vegetable oil{1/4%cup}, and @milk{1/2%cup}. Combine wet and dry, then stir in @hot water{1/2%cup} until smooth.

Pour into a greased 9" #springform pan{} lined with parchment. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for ~{25%minutes} to ~{30%minutes}. Cool completely.

= Coffee Syrup =

Combine @water{1/4%cup}, @sugar{2%tbsp}, and @instant coffee{1%tsp} in a small #saucepan{}. Bring to a boil, remove from heat.

Brush over cooled cake.

= Chocolate Mousse =

Heat @heavy cream{1 1/3%cups} to about 150°F. Pour over chopped @dark chocolate{7%oz}(70%). Let sit 1 minute, stir until smooth. Cool to room temperature, refrigerate ~{30%minutes}. Whisk until light and fluffy.

Whip remaining @cold heavy cream{1%cup} with @sugar{1%tbsp} and @vanilla{1%tsp} to soft peaks. Fold into chocolate.

Spread over cake and refrigerate at least ~{2%hours}.

= Chocolate Mirror Glaze =

Bring @water{1/2%cup}, @sugar{1%cup}, and @glucose{1%tbsp} to a boil.

Sift @cocoa powder{1/4%cup} into a bowl, whisk in hot syrup. Add @gelatin{7%g}, stir until dissolved.

Cool to 30°C. Pour over chilled mousse cake. Let set before slicing.

data.title: Cinnamon Buns
data.cook_source:
---
title: Cinnamon Buns
servings: 12
tags:
  - Baking
  - Breakfast
  - Pastry
---

= Dough =

In a large bowl whisk @all-purpose flour{4%cups}, @granulated sugar{1/2%cup}, and @fine sea salt{3/4%tsp}. Add @unsalted butter{1/3%cup}(softened) and work in with forks or fingers.

Warm @whole milk{1%cup} to about 100°F, whisk in @instant yeast{2.25%tsp}. Mix flour mixture with milk and @eggs{2} and @egg yolk{1} until a loose dough forms.

Knead on floured surface ~{5%minutes} to ~{8%minutes} until smooth. Place in greased bowl, cover, rise ~{90%minutes} until doubled.

= Filling =

Whisk @Demerara sugar{1%cup} and @ground cinnamon{2.5%tbsp} together. Set aside.

= Assembly and Baking =

Roll dough into a rectangle about 27" x ¼" thick. Spread @unsalted butter{1/3%cup}(softened) and sprinkle cinnamon sugar evenly.

Roll into a log, cut into 2" rolls. Place in #baking tray{}, rest ~{30%minutes} to ~{45%minutes} until 1.5x size.

Bake at 375°F (190°C) for ~{15%minutes} to ~{20%minutes}.

Whisk @cream cheese{4%oz}(softened) until fluffy, add @powdered sugar{3/4%cup}, then @whole milk{2%tbsp} to ~{3%tbsp} and @vanilla bean{1/2}. Pour glaze over buns.

data.title: Creamy Cajun Chicken Pasta
data.cook_source:
---
title: Creamy Cajun Chicken Pasta
servings: 4
tags:
  - Pasta
  - Chicken
  - Cajun
---

Cook @linguine pasta{8%oz} according to package. Reserve pasta water.

Cut @boneless chicken thighs{4} into small pieces. Season with @cajun seasoning{1 1/2%tbsp} and fry in @olive oil{2%tsp} in a #pan{} until cooked. Remove to a bowl.

Add @unsalted butter{4%tbsp} to the same #pan{}. Sauté @garlic{5%cloves}(minced) until aromatic. Add @tomato paste{1%tbsp} and fry for one minute.

Add @heavy whipping cream{1 1/2%cup}, more @cajun seasoning{} to taste, and @parmesan cheese{1/2%cup}(grated). Bring to a simmer.

Add the cooked chicken and cooked pasta to the sauce and toss until combined and warmed through. Add reserved pasta water if desired to thin. Serve with @parmesan cheese{}(freshly grated) and @parsley{2%tbsp}(finely chopped).

data.title: Crispy Calamari
data.cook_source:
---
title: Crispy Calamari
servings: 2
tags:
  - Seafood
  - Appetizer
  - Fried
---

Skin, rinse, and cut @squid{2}(whole) into rings. Place in a bowl with @lemon juice{}(squeeze), @bicarbonate of soda{3%tsp}, @salt{}(large pinch), @egg{1}, and @all-purpose flour{1%tbsp}. Mix well and leave to tenderise in the fridge for at least ~{1%hour}.

Heat @vegetable oil{} to 180°C (355°F) in a heavy #pan{}. Coat squid in @panko breadcrumbs{} in batches.

Fry for ~{3%minutes} to ~{5%minutes} until crisp and golden. Season with salt and serve hot with your favourite sauce.

data.title: Pumpkin Pie
data.cook_source:
---
title: Pumpkin Pie
servings: 8
tags:
  - Dessert
  - Pie
  - Thanksgiving
---

= Pumpkin Puree (or use 15 oz can) =

Cut top and bottom off @sugar pumpkin{1}, halve, remove seeds.

Brush with oil, roast on foil-lined sheet at 400°F (205°C) for ~{40%minutes} until soft.

Remove skins, blend until smooth. If watery, boil off in a #pan{}.

= Crust =

Combine @all-purpose flour{2%cups}, @granulated sugar{1%tbsp}, and @fine sea salt{1%tsp}. Add @cold unsalted butter{1%cup}(cubed), mix to pea-sized pieces.

Drizzle in @ice water{} until cohesive.

Flatten to 4" disk, wrap, chill ~{20%minutes}.

Roll to ¼" thick, fit into 9" #pie dish{}, trim and crimp. Chill briefly.

= Filling =

Whisk together pumpkin puree, @sweetened condensed milk{1%can}, @brown sugar{1/2%cup}(packed), @eggs{2} plus @egg yolk{1}, @fine sea salt{1/2%tsp}, @ground ginger{1%tsp}, @ground cinnamon{1 1/2%tsp}, @ground cloves{1/4%tsp}, @nutmeg{1/4%tsp}(grated), and @vanilla extract{2%tsp}. Pour into crust.

Bake 10 minutes at 400°F, then 35–40 minutes at 375°F until set with slight jiggle.

Cool to room temperature, chill ~{4%hours}. Top with whipped cream and cinnamon or nutmeg.

data.title: Sticky Toffee Pudding
data.cook_source:
---
title: Sticky Toffee Pudding
servings: 12
tags:
  - Dessert
  - Holiday
  - Baking
---

= Cake =

Bring @pitted dates{1%cup}(roughly chopped) and @water{1%cup}(boiling) to a boil in a small #pot{}, then simmer until soft. Add @baking soda{1/2%tsp} and puree until smooth.

In a bowl combine @all-purpose flour{1.5%cups}, @baking powder{1/4%tsp}, @fine sea salt{3/4%tsp}, and @nutmeg{1/2%tsp}(freshly grated). In a large bowl whisk @unsalted butter{6%tbsp}(softened) until creamy, add @light brown sugar{3/4%cup}(packed) and beat until light and fluffy, ~{3%minutes} to ~{5%minutes}. Add @eggs{2} one at a time, @egg yolk{1}, @vanilla bean paste{2%tsp}, and date puree. Fold in flour in two batches until just combined.

Pour into a greased 9x13" #baking dish{} and bake at 350°F (177°C) for ~{25%minutes} to ~{30%minutes} until a cake tester comes out clean. Poke holes all over with a skewer or fork.

= Toffee Sauce =

In a #saucepan{} combine @light brown sugar{1%cup}(packed), @unsalted butter{1/2%cup}, @light corn syrup{1/3%cup}, @molasses{2%tbsp}, and @heavy whipping cream{1%cup}. Bring to a boil and cook ~{4%minutes}.

Reduce and cook for ~{15%minutes} to ~{20%minutes} until deep amber.

Add remaining @heavy whipping cream{1%cup} and cook ~{3%minutes} to ~{4%minutes}. Pour sauce over cake. Slice and serve with ice cream.
